stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/AI domains such as transformers and text-generation.
The presence of 'llama' and 'safetensors' indicates relevance to modern large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer, llama, and AI/ML communities.

stories.title: Google's SynthID AI watermarking tech is being adopted by OpenAI, Nvidia, and more
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google's SynthID watermarking technology is being adopted by major AI players including OpenAI and Nvidia.
The technology aims to combat the proliferation of AI-generated misinformation by providing provenance for content.
This adoption signals a growing industry focus on AI content authenticity and detection.

stories.title: In stunning display of stupid, secret CISA credentials found in public GitHub repo
stories.author: Lee Hutchinson
bullet_points: Sensitive credentials (SSH keys, plaintext passwords) were exposed in a public GitHub repository.
The exposed data included CISA credentials, indicating a high-value security breach.
The vulnerability has been present in the public repository since November 2025.

stories.title: Toto 2.0: Time Series Forecasting Enters the Scaling Era
stories.author: David Asker
bullet_points: Introduction of Toto 2.0, a family of open-weights time series foundation models.
Demonstrates that time series models can scale effectively, showing performance gains across parameter sizes (4M to 2.5B).
Achieves new state-of-the-art results on multiple forecasting benchmarks, including GIFT-Eval and the contamination-resistant TIME benchmark.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash
stories.author: spectraldrift
bullet_points: Google has released Gemini 3.5 Flash, indicating a new iteration in their large language model lineup.
The release targets API integration, suggesting a focus on developer accessibility and real-world application.
The 'Flash' designation implies optimization for speed and efficiency, positioning it for high-throughput tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model trending on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', indicating its primary function as a large language model.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it within the mainstream ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and performance improvements.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to democratize access to advanced AI safety tooling.

stories.title: It’s make or break time for AI labeling systems
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: The article focuses on the critical testing phase for AI labeling systems designed to combat deepfakes and synthetic media.
Two key technologies, SynthID and C2PA Content Credentials, are undergoing major expansion to improve content provenance tracking.
The core theme is the industry's push to verify the origin of digital media, which is a major challenge in the current AI landscape.

stories.title: If Google can’t make AI agents useful, maybe no one can
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: The article discusses the long-standing gap between AI promises (capable assistants) and current reality (clueless interns).
The open-source platform OpenClaw is highlighted as a recent catalyst for improving AI agent functionality.
Major AI labs are now actively competing to replicate the success seen with platforms like OpenClaw.

stories.title: When Does Model Collapse Occur in Structured Interactive Learning?
stories.author: Weijie Su
bullet_points: The rise of generative AI creates interactive learning environments where models are trained on synthetic data from other models.
This paradigm challenges classical statistical learning by moving away from training data exclusively drawn from the target population.
The paper addresses the critical issue of 'model collapse' within these continuously updated, interactive learning systems.

stories.title: TIDE: Efficient and Lossless MoE Diffusion LLM Inference with I/O-aware Expert Offload
stories.author: Yuzhang Shang
bullet_points: Introduces TIDE, a method for efficient and lossless inference of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Diffusion LLMs (dLLMs).
Addresses the deployment challenge of large-scale dLLMs on resource-constrained devices by optimizing I/O and compute.
Positions dLLMs as a competitive alternative to traditional autoregressive (AR) models, leveraging parallel block-level decoding.

stories.title: From Seeing to Thinking: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning Improves Post-Training of Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Yuyin Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language Models (VLMs): performance is often bottlenecked by visual perception rather than reasoning ability.
The study proposes a novel training methodology by decoupling VLM capabilities into three distinct stages: visual perception, visual reasoning, and textual reasoning.
This decomposition aims to systematically improve VLM post-training by specializing the training for each core capability.

stories.title: ClinSeekAgent: Automating Multimodal Evidence Seeking for Agentic Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Yuyin Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces ClinSeekAgent, an automated framework designed for dynamic multimodal evidence seeking in clinical settings.
Addresses a key limitation in current LLM applications for clinical support: the assumption that evidence is pre-curated.
Shifts the paradigm to require agents to actively plan, seek, and synthesize heterogeneous evidence from real-world sources.

stories.title: ThoughtTrace: Understanding User Thoughts in Real-World LLM Interactions
stories.author: Tianmin Shu
bullet_points: Introduces ThoughtTrace, a novel, large-scale dataset designed to capture users' internal thoughts during LLM interactions, moving beyond just transcribed dialogue.
The dataset pairs real-world multi-turn conversations with self-reported user thoughts, including reasons for prompting and reactions to AI responses.
ThoughtTrace was collected across 20 different language models, providing valuable, nuanced data for future conversational AI research.

stories.title: Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment
stories.author: healsdata
bullet_points: A significant shift is occurring in European payment infrastructure.
130 million Europeans are reportedly moving away from Visa and Mastercard.
The transition is toward a 'sovereign payment' system, indicating a major industry change.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source model serving (Ollama) and proprietary models (Anthropic).

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Trump Mobile may be leaking customer addresses
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Trump Mobile is facing accusations of insecurely storing customer data, potentially leaking addresses and phone numbers.
The alleged data leak also revealed the actual number of T1 Phone orders, contradicting viral claims.
The story is timely, occurring close to the rumored release of the T1 Phone.

stories.title: The biggest data center ever is becoming a huge problem in Utah
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: A massive 40,000-acre data center, the Stratos Project, is planned for Box Elder County, Utah.
The project aims to bolster American AI dominance.
The development faces significant opposition from experts and public backlash.

stories.title: Android 17 is getting its own version of Apple’s Handoff
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Google is introducing a new Android feature called "Continue On" to mimic Apple's Handoff functionality.
This feature allows users to start a task on an Android phone and seamlessly continue it on a compatible tablet.
Initially, the feature will only support tasks moving from the phone to the tablet, with bidirectional support planned for the future.

stories.title: Samsung workers set to strike at worst possible time
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Samsung Electronics faces an 18-day strike involving over 47,000 domestic chipmaking workers.
The strike is triggered by the collapse of negotiations over bonus payments between the company and its union.
The action raises concerns about memory chip production amid existing industry shortages.

stories.title: Mercedes’ electric AMG GT 4-door coupe can go 0-60 in 2 seconds
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Mercedes has unveiled its new electric AMG GT 4-door coupe, signaling a push into ultra-high performance EVs.
The vehicle boasts impressive performance, capable of reaching 0-60 mph in just 2 seconds.
The production model incorporates technology previously showcased in the automaker's high-mileage XX concept.

stories.title: China banned RTX 5090D V2 while Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was visiting
stories.author: Zijing Wu in Hong Kong and Michael Acton in San Francisco
bullet_points: A specific high-end GPU, the RTX 5090D V2, has been banned from entry into China.
The ban was enforced at Chinese customs checkpoints.
The incident occurred while Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was reportedly visiting China.

stories.title: The Internet can't stop watching Figure AI's humanoid robots handling packages
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Figure AI is publicly showcasing its humanoid robots performing tasks, such as handling packages.
The livestream highlights a growing public fascination or 'soft spot' for humanoid robotics.
This signals increasing real-world deployment and public interest in advanced humanoid AI systems.

stories.title: FBI seeks US-wide access to license plate cameras, wants "data in near real time"
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FBI is seeking nationwide access to license plate camera data.
The agency plans to contract vendors to facilitate tracking and searching of vehicles across the US.
A key requirement is the ability to access this data in 'near real time'.

stories.title: "I'll buy 10 of those"—NASA science chief yearns for mass-produced satellites
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: NASA's science chief is advocating for the mass production of satellites to increase scientific data collection in space.
The core challenge identified is the current limitation in deploying sufficient numbers of scientific instruments.
This trend points toward a potential industry shift toward more accessible and scalable space infrastructure.

stories.title: Two AI-based science assistants succeed with drug-retargeting tasks
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Two new AI-based science assistants have demonstrated success in drug-retargeting tasks.
The tools are capable of generating novel scientific hypotheses.
One of the assistants further progressed by analyzing associated data.

stories.title: A Methodology for Selecting and Composing Runtime Architecture Patterns for Production LLM Agents
stories.author: Vasundra Srinivasan
bullet_points: Introduces the 'stochastic-deterministic boundary' (SDB) as a critical architectural concept for production LLM agents.
The SDB defines a formal contract (proposer, verifier, commit, reject) governing how LLM outputs translate into system actions.
Argues that the SDB is the fundamental, load-bearing primitive for building robust, real-world LLM agent runtimes.

stories.title: HaorFloodAlert: Deseasonalized ML Ensemble for 72-Hour Flood Prediction in Bangladesh Haor Wetlands
stories.author: Md. Zakir Hossen
bullet_points: Introduces HaorFloodAlert, a novel ML ensemble designed for 72-hour flood prediction in Bangladesh's Haor wetlands.
Addresses a critical gap in current flood models by accounting for the unique, flat, backwater dynamics of these basins, unlike traditional riverine flood models.
Highlights the technical challenge of deseasonalization, noting that temperature data previously skewed accuracy in the prediction model.

stories.title: Not Every Rubric Teaches Equally: Policy-Aware Rubric Rewards for RLVR
stories.author: Yunzhong He
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in rubric-based rewards for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).
Standard rubric aggregation conflates a criterion's inherent human importance with its actual utility as an optimization signal.
The proposed method introduces 'Policy-Aware Rubric Rewards' to better weight criteria during training.

stories.title: Rethinking Visual Attribution for Chest X-ray Reasoning in Large Vision Language Models
stories.author: Aidong Zhang
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of visual attribution in Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) used for medical tasks like Chest X-ray reasoning.
A key concern is that current visual explanations may not accurately reflect the actual visual evidence used by the model, impacting clinical trustworthiness.
The paper tackles the challenge of verifying model reasoning when ground-truth annotations for internal decision-making are unavailable.

stories.title: Beyond Prediction Accuracy: Target-Space Recovery Profiles for Evaluating Model-Brain Alignment
stories.author: Kaoru Amano
bullet_points: Introduces a new framework to evaluate AI model-brain alignment beyond simple prediction accuracy.
The framework focuses on identifying which specific dimensions of the target brain's response space are recovered by the model.
Uses repeated fMRI measurements to validate the unified evaluation method for both model-brain and brain-brain alignment.

stories.title: Multi-axis Analysis of Image Manipulation Localization
stories.author: Bryan A. Plummer
bullet_points: The proliferation of advanced generative AI makes image manipulation increasingly accessible and convincing.
Manipulated images pose a growing threat by enabling the spread of misinformation and influencing public opinion.
The research addresses a gap in detecting advanced manipulations across various visual domains.

stories.title: HaorFloodAlert: Deseasonalized ML Ensemble for 72-Hour Flood Prediction in Bangladesh Haor Wetlands
stories.author: Md. Zakir Hossen
bullet_points: Introduces HaorFloodAlert, a novel ML ensemble designed specifically for predicting floods in Bangladesh's Haor wetlands.
Addresses a critical gap in current flood prediction models, which are often designed for riverine systems and fail to capture backwater dynamics in flat basins.
The model incorporates deseasonalization techniques to overcome the confounding influence of temperature on flood probability forecasting.

stories.title: Interpretable Computer Vision for Defect Detection in X-ray Tomography of Aerospace SiC/SiC Composites
stories.author: Thomas Philippe
bullet_points: Addresses the 'black-box' problem in deep learning applied to industrial inspection.
Focuses on improving defect detection in X-ray tomography of critical aerospace SiC/SiC composites.
Introduces p-ResNet-50, a novel convolutional framework designed to provide interpretable computer vision.

stories.title: SAGE: Scalable Automatic Gating Ensemble for Confident Negative Harvesting in Fraud Detection
stories.author: Amit Goyal
bullet_points: Addresses music streaming fraud, a growing issue impacting royalty payments and chart integrity.
Introduces SAGE, a novel counterfactual-aware negative harvesting ensemble for detection.
Tackles the challenge of distinguishing sophisticated fraud from legitimate user behavior (e.g., super-fans).

stories.title: KoRe: Compact Knowledge Representations for Large Language Models
stories.author: Jacopo Staiano
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inherent flaw in LLMs encoding world knowledge solely within their opaque parameters.
It proposes 'KoRe,' a method for creating Compact Knowledge Representations to improve LLM knowledge handling.
The research contrasts LLMs' internal knowledge storage with the human-readable, structured nature of Knowledge Graphs.

stories.title: Rethinking Visual Attribution for Chest X-ray Reasoning in Large Vision Language Models
stories.author: Aidong Zhang
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of visual attribution in Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) used for medical imaging (Chest X-rays).
A key concern is that current visual explanations may not accurately reflect the actual visual evidence used by the model, impacting clinical trustworthiness.
The paper tackles the challenge of verifying model reasoning when ground-truth annotations for internal model decisions are scarce.

stories.title: MixRea: Benchmarking Explicit-Implicit Reasoning in Large Language Models
stories.author: Yanru Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces 'MixRea,' a new benchmark to test explicit-implicit reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Investigates if LLMs suffer from 'inattentional blindness,' similar to humans, by ignoring subtle contextual cues.
Addresses the growing concern over LLM reliability in high-stakes decision-making environments.

stories.title: BalanceRAG: Joint Risk Calibration for Cascaded Retrieval-Augmented Generation
stories.author: Zhiyuan Wang
bullet_points: Introduces 'BalanceRAG,' a method to improve LLM factuality by intelligently managing when to use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
Proposes a cascaded RAG approach where an LLM-only branch is attempted first, escalating to RAG only if the initial model is uncertain.
Addresses the issue of conservative calibration in staged RAG systems, aiming for better overall utility.

stories.title: CopT: Contrastive On-Policy Thinking with Continuous Spaces for General and Agentic Reasoning
stories.author: Wenke Lee
bullet_points: Introduces CopT, a new reasoning pipeline designed to improve LLM efficiency by addressing limitations in standard Chain-of-Thought (CoT).
CopT reverses the traditional CoT order, allowing models to access plausible answers before engaging in extended 'thinking' (performative reasoning).
The method utilizes 'Contrastive On-Policy Thinking with Continuous Spaces' to enhance general and agentic reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Meta blocks human rights accounts from reaching audiences in Arabia and the UAE
stories.author: giuliomagnifico
bullet_points: Meta is implementing content restrictions targeting specific regions.
The blocks specifically affect human rights-focused accounts.
The geographic areas impacted are Arabia and the UAE.

stories.title: Github: internal repositories have been accessed
stories.author: nitter.net via benjajaja
bullet_points: GitHub reported that internal repositories have been accessed.
This indicates a potential security incident or unauthorized access within GitHub's infrastructure.
The story is being discussed on Lobsters, suggesting it is a topic of interest within the developer community.

stories.title: GitHub Source Code Breach - TeamPCP Claims Access to Internal Source Code
stories.author: cybersecuritynews.com via mseri
bullet_points: A potential security incident involving GitHub's internal source code has been reported.
The claim of access to this source code is attributed to a group named TeamPCP.
The story originates from the Lobsters forum, indicating community discussion around the breach.

stories.title: AI isn't replacing junior devs. Your org chart is.
stories.author: dennistraub
bullet_points: The article reframes the debate around AI replacing junior developers by distinguishing between individual capability and organizational structure.
The core argument suggests that the true danger lies in flawed 'pipeline decisions' made due to misinterpreting AI's impact.
It shifts the focus from AI replacing people to AI potentially reshaping organizational design and hiring strategies.

stories.title: Per-User OAuth for AI Agents: Why It Matters and What to Look For
stories.author: dumebii
bullet_points: The article discusses the necessity of Per-User OAuth for AI agents.
This shift is driven by the advanced capabilities of AI agents interacting with personal data (e.g., Slack).
It serves as a guide for developers on what security considerations to prioritize when building these agents.
